visualGoal: Show a marine simmer that a real, satisfying control stack already exists as repurposed flight/DIY gear — throttle, rudder, DIY board, button box, VR — with verified product proof throughout, before they read the analog-axis caveat. No dedicated boat hardware exists, so lean the visual weight on the gear that IS verifiable (throttle/rudder/DIY/VR) and keep the helm/quadrant honest as text-only.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Most-recommended helm stand-in. Currently text + Amazon link only because no verified marine-context image. Needs a real photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid or before certification if the shortlist should show a helm.
Mentioned only as the pricier sibling to warn buyers off paying for unused FFB. Low priority; text-only is fine.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The classic solder-it-yourself Bridge Command board. Text + link only; the verified BU0836X (solderless sibling) carries the DIY visual instead. Source the A board photo if we want to show the exact classic-guide part.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic home marine-sim bridge bench at deep teal and amber harbor rim light, a plywood console mockup with open cutouts left for real product overlays (throttle quadrant, flight-stick rudder, DIY board panel), labeled wires visible, curator Gus Calder gesturing at the throttle position, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake boat wheel, invented marine controller, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: The gate that decides everything: analog axis support
content: Sim reads analog axes? -> wheel/throttle work proportionally
Keyboard-only sim? -> wheel = binary left/right, throttle = dead
Fix: vJoy / JoyToKey / Xpadder glue software
Rule: check the store page before buying anything
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center decision chart, large readable text, marine teal/amber palette, verified product photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Marine sim buy order
content: 2 Engine control: flight quadrant $60 or TWCS $130
5 Bridge switches: PXN CB1 $80 or BBI-32 $53
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays, marine palette

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult marine-sim curator Gus Calder in a home bridge-console bay, one hand on a labeled wiring loom feeding a DIY interface board, calm practical shipwright energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
